#Text=starlight
#Text=It was Louis and Harry's first Christmas while living together, so they went all out with the decorating.
#Text=A wreath was hung on their door, glowing reindeer littered the yard, and a humongous Christmas tree stood in front of their bay window.

#Text=They both insisted that everything was perfectly decorated, partly because they just adored Christmas that much, and they wanted to compete with their neighbors, but that's a story for another time.
#Text=The tree, however, wasn't completely finished yet.
#Text=Louis and Harry had gone all out when buying string lights, tinsel, and ornaments for the tree.
#Text=They'd spent hours taking the tiny ornaments out of their boxes, hanging them delicately on the tree.
#Text=The only thing left was the glistening tree topper they had found at the store that morning.
#Text=Harry demanded that they buy the biggest, brightest star that the store had to offer, and now it sat in Louis' hands, waiting to be put on the tree.
#Text=Louis offered to put the star on top of the tree, knowing how clumsy Harry could be.
#Text=But now as Louis was standing in front of the large tree, he realized he wasn't tall enough to reach the very top.
#Text=Harry would be able to reach it no problem, but Louis really wanted to be the one to place the tree topper.
#Text="Need a lift?"
#Text=Harry asked, giving Louis a cheeky grin.
#Text=Louis rolled his eyes, then tried to stand on his tip-toes to reach, "No, I can do it myself."
#Text=Harry watched Louis struggle with his eyebrow piqued, unable to stop smiling at his boyfriend's persistence.
#Text=Louis had leaned forward, nearly falling into the tree and tipping it over in order to reach the top.
#Text="You're going to break the tree or break yourself," Harry intervened, putting a hand on Louis' shoulder, "Come here, I'll hold you up."
#Text=Louis obliged, letting his boyfriend hoist him up under his arms just far enough to reach the top of the tree.
#Text=Louis held his balance, being held in Harry's strong arms, as he carefully slid the base of the star over the top branch of the tree.
#Text=Once he straightened out the tree topper, Harry let him back down to the floor so they could both admire their hard work.
#Text="I think we did a pretty good job," Louis said, resting his head on the side of Harry's arm.
#Text="You couldn't have done it without me," Harry teased, and Louis playfully scoffed, "I love you, even though you're unbelievably small."
#Text=Louis shook his head, but he was grinning, "I love you even though you bully me for my height."
#Text=Then Harry kissed Louis on the forehead, and they moved on to wrapping the gifts for their family and friends, more than happy to be spending the holiday together.
